long-title: People find it tricky to do things like reading, counting looking after money.

sections.long-title: What is the level being targeted?

content.choice-subtitle: Things that happen between individuals, like one-to-one support or care

content.long-title: Who should be involved?
content: Patients
People with lower reading, counting and money skills
People from vulnerable groups like disabled people
People experiencing homelessness
Staff

content.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: Some people have lower skills
Some people can't speak English
Some staff have low skills or are not well equipped to support people

content.long-title: What would help to solve the problem?
content: Education in reading, writing, counting and money skills for people who want to improve in these areas
Staff training about supporting people with reading, counting and looking after money

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Finding or creating education courses
Setting up reading groups
Training staff to help people to use the health and benefits systems
Making information in accessible formats

content.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: Face to face support
Reading groups delivered in community settings
Information shared through TVs in waiting rooms and online videos

content.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: People having better understanding of reading, writing, counting and money management
People can use health and social care systems
People can understand more about their health
More staff have been trained

content.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: People report improved health
People report feeling less stressed
People feel healthier
People have fewer worries about money
People are happier with the services they receive

content.long-title: What will have changed in 1 year?
content: More staff trained and supporting people
People starting to use health and social care systems better
People start to feel less stressed and more confident managing money

content.long-title: What will have happened in 3 years?
content: People feel healthier and less stressed
People manage their health conditions better
People have fewer worries about money
People live healthier lives
People will have improved health literacy skills

content.choice-subtitle: Things that happen in organisations or communities, like teamwork or service coordination
content.long-title: What would help?
Who should be involved?
content: GP practices
Hospitals
Community centres
Adult education organisations
Adult skills teachers
Charities who support these people
Voluntary groups who support these people
Schools

content.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: People leave school without the skills they need
Some staff have low skills or awareness of health literacy challenges

content.long-title: What would help to solve the problem?
content: Teachers who can teach reading, counting, writing and money skills
Teaching reading, writing, counting and money skills to staff
Teachers for speakers of other languages able to teach reading, counting and money skills alongside English

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Training staff within organisations to support people
Training staff to use tools to check service users' understanding e.g. Teach Back; Chunk & Check
Training staff to use readability tools
Organisations offering staff training to build staff reading, writing, counting and money skills
Training staff in health and local authority organisations to teach relevant reading, counting and money skills

content.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: Accredited training sessions which can be accessed Face-to-Face or online
Staff engaged in health literacy training

content.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: Patients and clients have better understanding of health
Patients and clients have better understanding of local authority and social services systems
Increased patient and client satisfaction
Greater trust between patients/clients and health and local authority organisations

content.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: Number of people who have been trained
Patient satisfaction

content.long-title: What will have changed in 1 year?
content: Training courses will be in place
Health and local authority/social care organisations will know about health literacy
More organisations will be aware of the issues
Organisations will offer a wider range of ways of getting information e.g. using TVs in waiting rooms

content.long-title: What will have happened in 3 years?
content: People will be happier with the services they receive
People will make better use of appointments
Fewer people will not turn up to their appointments

content.choice-subtitle: Big system-wide things, like national policies or data systems
content.long-title: What would help?
Who should be involved?
content: Local councils

content.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: People with low skills can't understand and use health and local authority systems

content.long-title: What would help to solve the problem?
content: Make sure health and local authority systems can be used by people at all skills levels

content.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Organisational efforts to ensure people with all skill levels can read and use materials

content.long-title: What will have happened in 3 years?
content: People will be using health and social care systems better

sections.long-title: What are the current activities in this area?

content.long-title: Project one!
Project two!
